name: AppCheck.new
desc: Creates or returns the cached AppCheck instance for the given app.

name: app
desc: The Firebase app this service is associated with.

name: createToken
desc:
Creates a new AppCheckToken that can be sent
back to a client.

name: delete
desc: Cleans up resources used by this service.

name: verifyToken
desc:
Verifies a Firebase App Check token (JWT). If the token is valid, the promise is
fulfilled with the token's decoded claims; otherwise, the promise is
rejected.

name: values
desc: A constant List of the values in this enum, in order of their declaration.

name: token
desc: The Firebase App Check token.

name: ttlMillis
desc: The time-to-live duration of the token in milliseconds.

name: ttlMillis
desc:
The length of time, in milliseconds, for which the App Check token will
be valid. This value must be between 30 minutes and 7 days, inclusive.

name: appId
desc:
The App ID corresponding to the App the App Check token belonged to.
This value is not actually one of the JWT token claims. It is added as a
convenience, and is set as the value of the DecodedAppCheckToken.sub property.

name: aud
desc:
The audience for which this token is intended.
This value is a JSON array of two strings, the first is the project number of your
Firebase project, and the second is the project ID of the same project.

name: exp
desc:
The App Check token's expiration time, in seconds since the Unix epoch. That is, the
time at which this App Check token expires and should no longer be considered valid.

name: iat
desc:
The App Check token's issued-at time, in seconds since the Unix epoch. That is, the
time at which this App Check token was issued and should start to be considered
valid.

name: iss
desc:
The issuer identifier for the issuer of the response.
This value is a URL with the format
https://firebaseappcheck.googleapis.com/<PROJECT_NUMBER>, where <PROJECT_NUMBER> is the
same project number specified in the DecodedAppCheckToken.aud property.

name: sub
desc:
The Firebase App ID corresponding to the app the token belonged to.
As a convenience, this value is copied over to the appId property.

name: FirebaseAppCheckException
desc: Firebase App Check error code structure. This extends PrefixedFirebaseError.

name: consume
desc:
To use the replay protection feature, set this to true. The AppCheck.verifyToken
method will mark the token as consumed after verifying it.

name: alreadyConsumed
desc:
Indicates weather this token was already consumed.
If this is the first time AppCheck.verifyToken method has seen this token,
this field will contain the value false. The given token will then be
marked as already_consumed for all future invocations of this AppCheck.verifyToken
method for this token.

name: appId
desc: The App ID corresponding to the App the App Check token belonged to.

name: token
desc: The decoded Firebase App Check token.

name: ActionCodeSettings
desc:
This is the interface that defines the required continue/state URL with
optional Android and iOS bundle identifiers.

name: android
desc:
Defines the Android package name. This will try to open the link in an
android app if it is installed. If installApp is passed, it specifies
whether to install the Android app if the device supports it and the app is
not already installed. If this field is provided without a packageName, an
error is thrown explaining that the packageName must be provided in
conjunction with this field. If minimumVersion is specified, and an older
version of the app is installed, the user is taken to the Play Store to
upgrade the app.

name: handleCodeInApp
desc:
Whether to open the link via a mobile app or a browser.
The default is false. When set to true, the action code link is sent
as a Universal Link or Android App Link and is opened by the app if
installed. In the false case, the code is sent to the web widget first
and then redirects to the app if installed.

name: iOS
desc:
Defines the iOS bundle ID. This will try to open the link in an iOS app if it
is installed.

name: linkDomain
desc:
Defines the link domain to use for the current link. This can be a custom
domain configured in your Firebase project or a Firebase Dynamic Link domain.
If none is provided, the oldest configured domain is used by default.

name: url
desc:
Defines the link continue/state URL, which has different meanings in
different contexts:

name: installApp
desc:
Whether to install the Android app if the device supports it and the app is
not already installed.

name: minimumVersion
desc:
The Android minimum version if available. If the installed app is an older
version, the user is taken to the GOogle Play Store to upgrade the app.

name: packageName
desc:
Defines the required Android package name of the app where the link should be
handled if the Android app is installed.

name: bundleId
desc:
Defines the required iOS bundle ID of the app where the link should be
handled if the application is already installed on the device.

name: AllowByDefaultSmsRegionConfig
desc:
Defines a policy of allowing every region by default and adding disallowed
regions to a disallow list.

name: disallowedRegions
desc:
Two letter unicode region codes to disallow as defined by
https://cldr.unicode.org/

name: AllowlistOnlySmsRegionConfig
desc:
Defines a policy of only allowing regions by explicitly adding them to an
allowlist.

name: allowedRegions
desc:
Two letter unicode region codes to allow as defined by
https://cldr.unicode.org/

name: Auth
desc:
Auth service bound to the provided app.
An Auth instance can have multiple tenants.

name: Auth.new
desc: Creates or returns the cached Auth instance for the given app.

name: createCustomToken
desc:
Creates a new Firebase custom token (JWT) that can be sent back to a client
device to use to sign in with the client SDKs' signInWithCustomToken()
methods. (Tenant-aware instances will also embed the tenant ID in the
token.)

name: createProviderConfig
desc:
Returns a promise that resolves with the newly created AuthProviderConfig
when the new provider configuration is created.

name: createSessionCookie
desc:
Creates a new Firebase session cookie with the specified options. The created
JWT string can be set as a server-side session cookie with a custom cookie
policy, and be used for session management. The session cookie JWT will have
the same payload claims as the provided ID token.

name: createUser
desc: Creates a new user.

name: delete
desc: Cleans up resources used by this service.

name: deleteProviderConfig
desc:
Deletes the provider configuration corresponding to the provider ID passed.
If the specified ID does not exist, an auth/configuration-not-found error
is thrown.

name: deleteUser
desc: Deletes an existing user.

name: deleteUsers
desc: Deletes the users specified by the given uids.

name: generateEmailVerificationLink
desc:
Generates the out of band email action link to verify the user's ownership
of the specified email. The ActionCodeSettings object provided
as an argument to this method defines whether the link is to be handled by a
mobile app or browser along with additional state information to be passed in
the deep link, etc.

name: generatePasswordResetLink
desc:
Generates the out of band email action link to reset a user's password.
The link is generated for the user with the specified email address. The
optional ActionCodeSettings object
defines whether the link is to be handled by a mobile app or browser and the
additional state information to be passed in the deep link, etc.

name: generateSignInWithEmailLink
desc:
Generates the out of band email action link to verify the user's ownership
of the specified email. The ActionCodeSettings object provided
as an argument to this method defines whether the link is to be handled by a
mobile app or browser along with additional state information to be passed in
the deep link, etc.

name: generateVerifyAndChangeEmailLink
desc:
Generates an out-of-band email action link to verify the user's ownership
of the specified email. The ActionCodeSettings object provided
as an argument to this method defines whether the link is to be handled by a
mobile app or browser along with additional state information to be passed in
the deep link, etc.

name: getProviderConfig
desc:
Looks up an Auth provider configuration by the provided ID.
Returns a promise that resolves with the provider configuration
corresponding to the provider ID specified. If the specified ID does not
exist, an auth/configuration-not-found error is thrown.

name: getUser
desc: Gets the user data for the user corresponding to a given uid.

name: getUserByEmail
desc: Gets the user data for the user corresponding to a given email.

name: getUserByPhoneNumber
desc:
Gets the user data for the user corresponding to a given phone number. The
phone number has to conform to the E.164 specification.

name: getUserByProviderUid
desc: Gets the user data for the user corresponding to a given provider id.

name: getUsers
desc: Gets the user data corresponding to the specified identifiers.

name: importUsers
desc:
Imports the provided list of users into Firebase Auth.
A maximum of 1000 users are allowed to be imported one at a time.
When importing users with passwords,
UserImportOptions are required to be
specified.
This operation is optimized for bulk imports and will ignore checks on uid,
email and other identifier uniqueness which could result in duplications.

name: listProviderConfigs
desc:
Returns the list of existing provider configurations matching the filter
provided. At most, 100 provider configs can be listed at a time.

name: listUsers
desc:
Retrieves a list of users (single batch only) with a size of maxResults
starting from the offset as specified by pageToken. This is used to
retrieve all the users of a specified project in batches.

name: projectConfigManager
desc: The ProjectConfigManager instance associated with the current project.

name: revokeRefreshTokens
desc: Revokes all refresh tokens for an existing user.

name: setCustomUserClaims
desc:
Sets additional developer claims on an existing user identified by the
provided uid, typically used to define user roles and levels of
access. These claims should propagate to all devices where the user is
already signed in (after token expiration or when token refresh is forced)
and the next time the user signs in. If a reserved OIDC claim name
is used (sub, iat, iss, etc), an error is thrown. They are set on the
authenticated user's ID token JWT.

name: tenantManager
desc: The TenantManager instance associated with the current project.

name: updateProviderConfig
desc:
Returns a promise that resolves with the updated AuthProviderConfig
corresponding to the provider ID specified.
If the specified ID does not exist, an auth/configuration-not-found error
is thrown.

name: updateUser
desc: Updates an existing user.

name: verifyIdToken
desc:
Verifies a Firebase ID token (JWT). If the token is valid, the promise is
fulfilled with the token's decoded claims; otherwise, the promise is
rejected.

name: AuthClientErrorCode
desc: Auth client error codes and their default messages.

name: providerId

For an OIDC provider, this is always prefixed by oidc..

name: pageToken

The next page token. When not specified, the lookup starts from the beginning

Time, in seconds since the Unix epoch, when the end-user authentication

Whether or not the email of the user to whom the ID token belongs is

name: phoneNumber

name: DeleteUsersResult

Represents the result of the _BaseAuth.deleteUsers.
API.

A list of FirebaseArrayIndexError instances describing the errors that

name: failureCount
desc: The number of user records that failed to be deleted (possibly zero).

name: successCount
desc:
The number of users that were deleted successfully (possibly zero).
Users that did not exist prior to calling deleteUsers() are
considered to be successfully deleted.

name: SAMLAuthProviderConfig
desc:
The
SAML
Auth provider configuration interface. A SAML provider can be created via
_BaseAuth.createProviderConfig.

name: callbackURL
desc:
This is fixed and must always be the same as the OAuth redirect URL
provisioned by Firebase Auth,
https://project-id.firebaseapp.com/__/auth/handler unless a custom
authDomain is used.
The callback URL should also be provided to the SAML IdP during
configuration.

name: idpEntityId
desc: The SAML IdP entity identifier.

name: rpEntityId
desc:
The SAML relying party (service provider) entity ID.
This is defined by the developer but needs to be provided to the SAML IdP.

name: ssoURL
desc: The SAML IdP SSO URL. This must be a valid URL.

name: x509Certificates
desc:
The list of SAML IdP X.509 certificates issued by CA for this provider.
Multiple certificates are accepted to prevent outages during
IdP key rotation (for example ADFS rotates every 10 days). When the Auth
server receives a SAML response, it will match the SAML response with the
certificate on record. Otherwise the response is rejected.
Developers are expected to manage the certificate updates as keys are
rotated.

desc:
Tenant-aware Auth interface used for managing users, configuring SAML/OIDC providers,
generating email links for password reset, email verification, etc for specific tenants.

desc:
Creates a new Firebase custom token (JWT) that can be sent back to a client
device to use to sign in with the client SDKs' signInWithCustomToken()
methods. (Tenant-aware instances will also embed the tenant ID in the
token.)

when the new provider configuration is created.

session management (set as a server side session cookie with custom cookie policy).
The session cookie JWT will have the same payload claims as the provided ID token.

name: createUser

is thrown.

name: generateEmailVerificationLink
desc:
Generates the out of band email action link to verify the user's ownership
of the specified email. The ActionCodeSettings object provided
as an argument to this method defines whether the link is to be handled by a
mobile app or browser along with additional state information to be passed in
the deep link, etc.

name: generatePasswordResetLink
desc:
Generates the out of band email action link to reset a user's password.
The link is generated for the user with the specified email address. The
optional ActionCodeSettings object
defines whether the link is to be handled by a mobile app or browser and the
additional state information to be passed in the deep link, etc.

name: generateSignInWithEmailLink
desc:
Generates the out of band email action link to verify the user's ownership
of the specified email. The ActionCodeSettings object provided
as an argument to this method defines whether the link is to be handled by a
mobile app or browser along with additional state information to be passed in
the deep link, etc.

name: generateVerifyAndChangeEmailLink
desc:
Generates an out-of-band email action link to verify the user's ownership
of the specified email. The ActionCodeSettings object provided
as an argument to this method defines whether the link is to be handled by a
mobile app or browser along with additional state information to be passed in
the deep link, etc.

name: getProviderConfig

corresponding to the provider ID specified. If the specified ID does not
exist, an auth/configuration-not-found error is thrown.

name: importUsers
desc:
Imports the provided list of users into Firebase Auth.
A maximum of 1000 users are allowed to be imported one at a time.
When importing users with passwords,
UserImportOptions are required to be
specified.
This operation is optimized for bulk imports and will ignore checks on uid,
email and other identifier uniqueness which could result in duplications.

name: listProviderConfigs
desc:
Returns the list of existing provider configurations matching the filter
provided. At most, 100 provider configs can be listed at a time.

name: listUsers
desc:
Retrieves a list of users (single batch only) with a size of maxResults
starting from the offset as specified by pageToken. This is used to
retrieve all the users of a specified project in batches.

name: revokeRefreshTokens
desc: Revokes all refresh tokens for an existing user.

name: setCustomUserClaims

Sets additional developer claims on an existing user identified by the
provided uid, typically used to define user roles and levels of
access. These claims should propagate to all devices where the user is
already signed in (after token expiration or when token refresh is forced)
and the next time the user signs in. If a reserved OIDC claim name
is used (sub, iat, iss, etc), an error is thrown. They are set on the
authenticated user's ID token JWT.

The tenant identifier corresponding to this TenantAwareAuth instance.
All calls to the user management APIs, OIDC/SAML provider management APIs, email link
generation APIs, etc will only be applied within the scope of this tenant.

corresponding to the provider ID specified.
If the specified ID does not exist, an auth/configuration-not-found error
is thrown.

name: algorithm
desc:
The password hashing algorithm identifier. The following algorithm
identifiers are supported:
SCRYPT, STANDARD_SCRYPT, HMAC_SHA512, HMAC_SHA256, HMAC_SHA1,
HMAC_MD5, MD5, PBKDF_SHA1, BCRYPT, PBKDF2_SHA256, SHA512,
SHA256 and SHA1.

name: blockSize
desc:
The block size (normally 8) of the hashing algorithm. Required for the
STANDARD_SCRYPT algorithm.

name: derivedKeyLength
desc:
The derived key length of the hashing algorithm. Required for the
STANDARD_SCRYPT algorithm.

name: key
desc:
The signing key used in the hash algorithm in buffer bytes.
Required by hashing algorithms SCRYPT, HMAC_SHA512, HMAC_SHA256,
HAMC_SHA1 and HMAC_MD5.

name: memoryCost
desc:
The memory cost required for SCRYPT algorithm, or the CPU/memory cost.
Required for STANDARD_SCRYPT algorithm.

name: parallelization
desc:
The parallelization of the hashing algorithm. Required for the
STANDARD_SCRYPT algorithm.

name: rounds
desc:
The number of rounds for hashing calculation.
Required for SCRYPT, MD5, SHA512, SHA256, SHA1, PBKDF_SHA1 and
PBKDF2_SHA256.

name: saltSeparator
desc:
The salt separator in buffer bytes which is appended to salt when
verifying a password. This is only used by the SCRYPT algorithm.

name: runTransaction
desc:
Executes the given updateFunction and commits the changes applied within
the transaction.
You can use the transaction object passed to 'updateFunction' to read and
modify Firestore documents under lock. You have to perform all reads
before before you perform any write.
Transactions can be performed as read-only or read-write transactions. By
default, transactions are executed in read-write mode.
A read-write transaction obtains a pessimistic lock on all documents that
are read during the transaction. These locks block other transactions,
batched writes, and other non-transactional writes from changing that
document. Any writes in a read-write transactions are committed once
'updateFunction' resolves, which also releases all locks.
If a read-write transaction fails with contention, the transaction is
retried up to five times. The updateFunction is invoked once for each
attempt.
Read-only transactions do not lock documents. They can be used to read
documents at a consistent snapshot in time, which may be up to 60 seconds
in the past. Read-only transactions are not retried.
Transactions time out after 60 seconds if no documents are read.
Transactions that are not committed within than 270 seconds are also
aborted. Any remaining locks are released when a transaction times out.

name: Timestamp
desc:
A Timestamp represents a point in time independent of any time zone or calendar,
represented as seconds and fractions of seconds at nanosecond resolution in UTC
Epoch time. It is encoded using the Proleptic Gregorian Calendar which extends
the Gregorian calendar backwards to year one. It is encoded assuming all minutes
are 60 seconds long, i.e. leap seconds are "smeared" so that no leap second table
is needed for interpretation. Range is from 0001-01-01T00:00:00Z to
9999-12-31T23:59:59.999999999Z. By restricting to that range, we ensure that we
can convert to and from RFC 3339 date strings.

desc: An interface for interacting with the Firebase Cloud Messaging service.

name: Messaging.new
desc: Creates or returns the cached Messaging instance for the given app.

name: app
desc: The app associated with this Messaging instance.

name: delete
desc: Cleans up resources used by this service.

name: send
desc: Sends the given message via FCM.

name: sendEach
desc: Sends each message in the given array via Firebase Cloud Messaging.

name: sendEachForMulticast
desc:
Sends the given multicast message to all the FCM registration tokens
specified in it.

name: subscribeToTopic
desc: Subscribes a list of registration tokens to an FCM topic.

name: unsubscribeFromTopic
desc: Unsubscribes a list of registration tokens from an FCM topic.

name: MessagingClientErrorCode
desc: Messaging client error codes and their default messages.

name: code
desc: The error code.

name: message
desc: The default error message.

name: values
desc: A constant List of the values in this enum, in order of their declaration.

name: MessagingTopicManagementResponse
desc:
Interface representing the server response from the
Messaging.subscribeToTopic and Messaging.unsubscribeFromTopic methods.

name: errors
desc:
An array of errors corresponding to the provided registration token(s). The
length of this array will be equal to failureCount.

name: failureCount
desc:
The number of registration tokens that could not be subscribed to the topic
and resulted in an error.

name: successCount
desc:
The number of registration tokens that were successfully subscribed to the
topic.

name: MessagingTopicResponse
desc: Interface representing the server response from the legacy {@link Messaging.sendToTopic} method.

name: MessagingTopicResponse.new
desc: Interface representing the server response from the legacy {@link Messaging.sendToTopic} method.

name: messageId
desc:
The message ID for a successfully received request which FCM will attempt to
deliver to all subscribed devices.

name: MulticastMessage
desc:
Payload for the Messaging.sendEachForMulticast method. The payload contains all the fields
in the BaseMessage type, and a list of tokens.

name: Notification
desc: A notification that can be included in Message.

name: Notification.new
desc: A notification that can be included in Message.

name: body
desc: The notification body

name: imageUrl
desc: URL of an image to be displayed in the notification.

name: title
desc: The title of the notification.

name: SendResponse
desc:
Interface representing the status of an individual message that was sent as
part of a batch request.

name: error
desc: An error, if the message was not handed off to FCM successfully.

name: messageId
desc:
A unique message ID string, if the message was handed off to FCM for
delivery.

name: success
desc:
A boolean indicating if the message was successfully handed off to FCM or
not. When true, the messageId attribute is guaranteed to be set. When
false, the error attribute is guaranteed to be set.

name: TokenMessage
desc: A message targeting a specific registration token.

name: TopicMessage
desc: A message targeting a topic.

name: WebpushConfig
desc: Represents the WebPush protocol options that can be included in a Message.

name: WebpushConfig.new
desc: Represents the WebPush protocol options that can be included in a Message.

name: data
desc: A collection of data fields.

name: fcmOptions
desc: Options for features provided by the FCM SDK for Web.

name: headers
desc: A collection of WebPush headers. Header values must be strings.

name: notification
desc: A WebPush notification payload to be included in the message.

name: WebpushFcmOptions
desc:
Represents options for features provided by the FCM SDK for Web
(which are not part of the Webpush standard).

name: WebpushFcmOptions.new
desc:
Represents options for features provided by the FCM SDK for Web
(which are not part of the Webpush standard).

name: link
desc:
The link to open when the user clicks on the notification.
For all URL values, HTTPS is required.

name: WebpushNotification
desc:
Represents the WebPush-specific notification options that can be included in
WebpushConfig. This supports most of the standard
options as defined in the Web Notification
specification.

name: actions
desc:
A list of notification actions representing the actions
available to the user when the notification is presented.

name: badge
desc:
URL of the image used to represent the notification when there is
not enough space to display the notification itself.

name: body
desc: Body text of the notification.

name: customData
desc: Arbitrary key/value payload.

name: data
desc:
Arbitrary data that you want associated with the notification.
This can be of any data type.

name: dir
desc:
The direction in which to display the notification. Must be one
of auto, ltr or rtl.

name: icon
desc: URL to the notification icon.

name: image
desc: URL of an image to be displayed in the notification.

name: lang
desc: The notification's language as a BCP 47 language tag.

name: renotify
desc:
A boolean specifying whether the user should be notified after a
new notification replaces an old one. Defaults to false.

name: requireInteraction
desc:
Indicates that a notification should remain active until the user
clicks or dismisses it, rather than closing automatically.
Defaults to false.

name: silent
desc:
A boolean specifying whether the notification should be silent.
Defaults to false.

name: tag
desc: An identifying tag for the notification.

name: timestamp
desc:
Timestamp of the notification. Refer to
https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/notification/timestamp
for details.

name: title
desc: Title text of the notification.

name: vibrate
desc:
A vibration pattern for the device's vibration hardware to emit
when the notification fires.

name: action
desc: An action available to the user when the notification is presented

name: icon
desc: Optional icon for a notification action.

name: title
desc: Title of the notification action.

name: values
desc: A constant List of the values in this enum, in order of their declaration.

name: values
desc: A constant List of the values in this enum, in order of their declaration.

name: RulesFile
desc:
A source file containing some Firebase security rules. The content includes raw
source code including text formatting, indentation and comments.

name: Ruleset
desc: A set of Firebase security rules.

name: RulesetMetadata
desc: Required metadata associated with a ruleset.

name: createTime
desc: Creation time of the Ruleset as a UTC timestamp string.

name: name
desc:
Name of the Ruleset as a short string. This can be directly passed into APIs
like SecurityRules.getRuleset and SecurityRules.deleteRuleset.

name: RulesetMetadataList
desc: A page of ruleset metadata.

name: nextPageToken
desc: The next page token if available. This is needed to retrieve the next batch.

name: rulesets
desc: A batch of ruleset metadata.

name: SecurityRules
desc: The Firebase SecurityRules service interface.

name: SecurityRules.new
desc: Creates or returns the cached SecurityRules instance for the given app.

name: app
desc: The Firebase app this service is associated with.

name: createRuleset
desc: Creates a new Ruleset from the given RulesFile.

name: delete
desc: Cleans up resources used by this service.

name: deleteRuleset
desc:
Deletes the Ruleset identified by the given
name. The input name should be the short name string without the project ID
prefix. For example, to delete the projects/project-id/rulesets/my-ruleset,
pass the  short name "my-ruleset". Rejects with a not-found error if the
specified Ruleset cannot be found.

name: getFirestoreRuleset
desc:
Gets the Ruleset currently applied to
Cloud Firestore. Rejects with a not-found error if no ruleset is applied
on Firestore.

name: getRuleset
desc:
Gets the Ruleset identified by the given
name. The input name should be the short name string without the project ID
prefix. For example, to retrieve the projects/project-id/rulesets/my-ruleset,
pass the short name "my-ruleset". Rejects with a not-found error if the
specified Ruleset cannot be found.

name: getStorageRuleset
desc:
Gets the Ruleset currently applied to a
Cloud Storage bucket. Rejects with a not-found error if no ruleset is applied
on the bucket.

name: listRulesetMetadata
desc: Retrieves a page of ruleset metadata.

name: releaseFirestoreRuleset
desc:
Applies the specified Ruleset ruleset
to Cloud Firestore.

name: releaseFirestoreRulesetFromSource
desc:
Creates a new Ruleset from the given
source, and applies it to Cloud Firestore.

name: releaseStorageRuleset
desc:
Applies the specified Ruleset ruleset
to a Cloud Storage bucket.

name: releaseStorageRulesetFromSource
desc:
Creates a new Ruleset from the given
source, and applies it to a Cloud Storage bucket.

name: packageVersion
desc: The current version of the package.
